conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have two children – Alex, who's 8, and Blake, who's 20. They both live with me here in Bristol.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have two children - Alex (age 8) and Blake (age 20), both living with you in Bristol.

To assess eligibility for each child, I need to ask about Blake specifically since they are 20 years old. 

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training, but stops once they turn 20 or leave education/training.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment – they finished college a while back and have just been figuring out what to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Blake is 20 and not in approved education or training, I have the information I need about Blake.

Now, regarding Alex who is 8 years old and living with you - just to confirm a few important details:

**Is anyone else (such as another parent or family member) currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Just one more question to complete the assessment:

**Are you currently living in the UK, and do you have the right to reside here?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in Bristol and I've always lived in the UK – I'm a British citizen.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility:**

✅ **Alex (age 8): You ARE eligible** - Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, you're responsible for them, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

❌ **Blake (age 20): You are NOT eligible** - Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit only continues past age 16 for young people under 20 who remain in approved education or training.

**What this means:** You can claim Child Benefit for Alex. If Blake were to return to approved education or training before their 20th birthday was reached, you could have potentially claimed for them during that time, but since they're already 20, that option is no longer available.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 20 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 20, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. You are a British citizen living in the UK. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Alex meets all the criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.

Blake is 20 years old and is not in approved education or training. According to the eligibility rules, Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. However, Blake has finished college and is not currently in any education or training. Since Blake is 20 years old and not in approved education or training, they do not meet the age and education criteria for Child Benefit eligibility.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex (age 8) as they are under 16 and live with you in the UK. However, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake (age 20) as they are over the age limit - Child Benefit only continues past age 16 for those under 20 who are in approved education or training, which Blake is not.
